name: The Hills of Jurançon Loop
description: This moderate 44.2-mile road cycling loop starting from Pau offers diverse scenery and 2829 feet of elevation gain in 3 hours 20 minutes.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Pau offer access to diverse terrain, from the foothills of the Pyrenees to the plains of the Gave de Pau river. The region features challenging mountain climbs, gentle riverside paths, and rolling hillsides. Cyclists encounter varied landscapes, including high mountain passes, river valleys, and vineyard-covered slopes. This area provides a range of road cycling experiences for different skill levels. Best road cycling routes in Pau The most popular road cycling route is L'Aubisque, The Tour Route, a 61.4 miles (98.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route features a significant climb in the Pyrenees, known from the Tour de France. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Small loop of Pau, a moderate 29.6 miles (47.6 km) path. This route offers a circuit around the city, providing views of the surrounding area. Local road cyclists also love the The Hills of Jurançon Loop, a 44.2 miles (71.1 km) trail leading through the Jurançon vineyards and rolling hills, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Road cycling around Pau is defined by the Pyrenees mountains, the Gave de Pau river, and the Jurançon vineyards. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside paths to demanding mountain ascents. The routes in Pau are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 28,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pau's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Pau?
A: The Pau region offers a vast network of over 1600 road cycling routes. These routes cater to all levels, with approximately 250 easy, over 1000 moderate, and nearly 300 difficult options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Pau?
A: Road cycling around Pau features highly diverse terrain. You can find challenging mountain climbs in the Pyrenees, gentle and scenic paths along the Gave de Pau river, and rolling hillsides through the picturesque Jurançon vineyards. The region offers everything from high mountain passes to river valleys and vineyard-covered slopes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Pau?
A: Yes, there are numerous options for beginners. Approximately 250 easy road cycling routes are available. These often follow flatter terrain, such as paths along the Gave de Pau river. An example of an easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5348401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont d'Assat Suspension Bridge – Pont de Nay loop from Pau&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I experience the famous Tour de France climbs near Pau?
A: Pau is a gateway to legendary Pyrenean climbs featured in the Tour de France. You can tackle iconic passes like the Col d'Aubisque and Col de Marie-Blanque. A challenging route that includes a significant Tour de France climb is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e854915039/l-aubisque-the-tour-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Aubisque, The Tour Route&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19221157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col du Soulor – Col d'Aubisque loop from Assat&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I cycle along the Gave de Pau river?
A: Yes, the Gave de Pau river offers dedicated and gentle cycle paths, providing refreshing rides suitable for various fitness levels. Routes like the Vélosud follow the river, mixing dedicated paths with secondary roads through fertile plains and charming medieval towns. You can find more information about the Vélosud from Pau towards Lourdes &lt;a href="https://en.francevelotourisme.com/cycle-route/the-velosud/pau-lourdes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;here&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there road cycling routes through the Jurançon vineyards?
A: Absolutely. The area around Pau features the picturesque Jurançon vineyards and rolling hills, offering a different kind of cycling experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e850030372/the-hills-of-jurancon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Hills of Jurançon Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular route that winds through this beautiful wine-growing region, providing moderate challenges and scenic views.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see while road cycling near Pau?
A: While cycling near Pau, you can encounter several historical and natural landmarks. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4119926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;National Museum and Estate of the Château de Pau&lt;/a&gt; is visible from certain routes. Other attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2185442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gave de Pau Cycle Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1109052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boulevard of the Pyrenees&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning views.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Pau?
A: The best time for road cycling in Pau, especially for tackling the Pyrenean passes, is typically from late spring (May) through early autumn (October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and the high mountain passes are clear of snow. Lower elevation routes along the river or through vineyards are enjoyable for a longer season.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Pau?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Pau are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e850791167/small-loop-of-pau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small loop of Pau&lt;/a&gt; offers a circuit around the city, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6984436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Château de Pau loop from Pau&lt;/a&gt; provides a moderate ride with scenic views.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Pau?
A: The road cycling routes in Pau are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 800 reviews. Reviewers often praise the region's diverse terrain, from challenging mountain ascents to gentle riverside paths, and the stunning Pyrenean scenery.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in the Pau area?
A: Yes, Pau offers several family-friendly road cycling options, particularly along the Gave de Pau river. These routes are generally flatter and safer, often utilizing dedicated cycle paths. The Vélosud, for example, provides accessible sections suitable for families.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling trails near Pau?
A: Parking availability varies by route. For routes starting directly in Pau, city center parking options are available. For routes venturing into the Pyrenees or specific areas like the Jurançon vineyards, trailheads or nearby villages often provide designated parking areas. It's advisable to check specific route details for recommended starting points and parking information.
tips: A flat road (it's pleasant in the area!) but bucolic between the vines.
A beautiful little road between pastures in a quiet and traffic-free environment
Beautiful views of Nay from the river bank

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Loop starting from Pau?
faqs.answer: The loop begins in Pau. You can typically find public parking within the city center or near the route's initial segments. Look for available street parking or public garages in Pau.

faqs.question: Is this racebike loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While not suitable for absolute beginners, cyclists with good fitness and some experience on longer rides with significant elevation gain (around 862 meters) should find it manageable. It's more challenging than an 'easy' route, which typically has less than 500 feet of elevation gain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain for this racebike loop is varied, primarily consisting of paved roads. Given its moderate difficulty and location near the Pyrenees, expect winding and hilly sections. While most of the route is paved, some moderate routes in the region might include short unpaved segments, though this is less common for racebike tours.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery, including views of the Pyrenees mountains and the Basque Country. You'll pass through picturesque areas and can experience highlights such as the Vineyard Amphitheater and Pont de Nay. Keep an eye out for natural features and charming villages.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this loop is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. While summer offers beautiful conditions, Pau can experience high temperatures, especially in July. If cycling in summer, it's advisable to start early in the morning to avoid extreme heat during the afternoon.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebike cycling on public roads. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, bringing a dog on a racebike route of this distance and difficulty might not be ideal for their safety or comfort, especially given the traffic and speed involved.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this loop. It uses public roads and paths around Pau.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Le piémont pyrénéen (18%), L'Aubisque, la route du Tour (17%), Les coteaux de Jurançon (14%), and Les coteaux béarnais (13%). You'll also find smaller segments of the Véloroute du Piémont Pyrénéen 01 (Pyrénées Atlantique) and the Via Sancti Martini - Chemin de Saragosse.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: As the route passes through various towns and villages, you will likely find opportunities for refreshments. Pau itself offers numerous options at the start or end, and smaller establishments may be available in the communities you pass through. It's always a good idea to carry some snacks and water, especially for a moderate-difficulty ride of this length.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: For this moderate racebike tour, you should bring your racebike in good condition, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water bottles, and energy snacks. Given the elevation changes, consider bringing spare tubes, a pump, and basic repair tools. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also recommended, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is a loop route, meaning it starts and ends in Pau, bringing you back to your starting point without retracing your steps.
